task_completion.description: Create a bold, centered title 'BUILDING FIRE SAFETY INSPECTION FORM'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Microsoft Word 2010 is open. The text 'BUILDING FIRE SAFETY INSPECTION FORM' is visible at the top of the document, formatted as bold and center-aligned.

task_completion.description: Create 'Building Information' section with 4 text form fields
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section titled 'Building Information' is present, containing labels for Building Name, Address, Inspector Name, and Inspection Date. Next to or below each label is a legacy text form field (typically appearing as a gray shaded rectangular box).

task_completion.description: Create 'Inspection Checklist' section with 8 checkbox form fields
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section titled 'Inspection Checklist' is present, listing fire safety items (e.g., Fire extinguisher, Exit signs). There are exactly 8 legacy checkbox form fields (gray shaded square boxes) corresponding to these items.

task_completion.description: Create 'Overall Assessment' section with a dropdown form field
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section titled 'Overall Assessment' is present, containing a legacy dropdown form field (gray shaded box). If the dropdown properties dialog is visible in any screenshot, it should show options like 'Pass' and 'Fail'.

task_completion.description: Create 'Comments' section with a text form field
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section titled 'Comments' is present, accompanied by a legacy text form field (gray shaded box) for user input.

task_completion.description: Protect the document for 'Filling in forms' only
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Restrict Formatting and Editing' pane is open on the right side of Word. The checkbox 'Allow only this type of editing in the document' is checked, and 'Filling in forms' is selected in the dropdown menu. The 'Start enforcing protection' button should be clicked (or the pane shows 'Stop Protection').

task_completion.description: Save the document to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'fire_inspection_form.docx' in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' directory, or the Word title bar displays 'fire_inspection_form.docx'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Microsoft Word 2010 GUI to create the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Word 2010 ribbon (specifically the Developer tab) and typing in the document, rather than using a terminal, PowerShell, or Python script to generate the .docx file.

integrity.description: The agent used Legacy Form Fields rather than modern content controls
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking the 'Legacy Tools' icon (a briefcase folder) in the Developer tab's Controls group to insert the text, checkbox, and dropdown fields.

integrity.description: Document protection was applied via the Word interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with the 'Restrict Editing' pane in the Word GUI to apply the forms protection, rather than modifying the document's underlying XML settings.xml file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The final document must be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\fire_inspection_form.docx. It must contain exactly 5 text input fields (4 for Building Information, 1 for Comments), 8 checkbox form fields for the inspection items, and 1 dropdown field. The document must be protected specifically for 'forms' mode. Note: The automated verifier strictly checks for 'Pass' and 'Fail' in the dropdown (ignoring 'Conditional Pass'), and looks for 'Fire extinguisher' (singular) in the checklist, so accept these variations if visible.
